---
applyTo: "**"
excludeAgent: "coding-agent"
---

# Copilot Code Review instructions

## Review priorities

- Identify correctness issues, edge cases, and potential regressions.
- Highlight security risks (secrets exposure, injection risks, authz/authn mistakes, unsafe deserialization, etc.).
- Flag likely CI failures based on repo conventions (Nx targets, TypeScript strictness, .NET build constraints).

## Repo-specific checks

- Nx monorepo: changes to `project.json`, `nx.json`, `tsconfig.base.json`, `eslint.config.mjs`, or `.github/workflows/**` are high-impact; review carefully.
- Domain boundaries: backends are bounded contexts (DDD); avoid introducing cross-domain dependencies (prefer shared libs under `libs/**` when needed).
- Secrets: treat `publish.ps1` and cache/CI configuration as sensitive.
- Frontend i18n: avoid breaking translation merge behavior (`merge-i18n.js`) and referenced i18n source locations.
- Angular bundle hygiene: flag changes that make the initial bundle load unnecessary feature code. Top-level XMate routes must lazy-load domain route files instead of statically importing/spreading route arrays; heavy child pages/widgets should use `loadComponent`, lazy widget loaders, or dynamic `import()` wrappers. Flag heavy providers added to `appConfig` when they can be route/component-scoped, eager dashboard widget component references, Syncfusion `*AllModule` imports where narrow modules + explicit services work, static imports of all locale JSON files, `import * as THREE` namespace imports, and non-`defer`red third-party scripts in `index.html`.
- Angular resources/httpResource: flag any `value()` read in templates, `computed()`, or component logic that is not guarded by `hasValue()` in the same control flow. In error state `value()` throws, and in reactive/template reevaluation paths this can cause console spam, severe slowdown, or UI freezes.
- Angular tabs: flag hardcoded per-tab `<button>` blocks and repeated `activeTab() === '<id>'` checks — tabs must come from a typed `tabs` config + `@for`. Buttons must use `btn btn-plain p-0` with `text-primary` + `text-decoration-underline` (active) and `text-gray-900` (inactive); flag `nav nav-underline` / `nav-link.active`. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Tabs".
- Angular grid sizing & scroll containment: flag fixed px/vh heights on `<ejs-grid>` / `<ejs-treegrid>` / `<ejs-gantt>` / containers, and flex chains missing `mh-0` upstream of the grid. For tabbed views (modal or page), flag missing `flex-shrink-0` on tabs nav or missing `flex-grow-1 mh-0` on the active-tab area inside a `d-flex flex-column h-100` wrapper — symptom is "tabs disappear when the grid is scrolled". See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Grid sizing & scroll containment".
- Angular grid defaults: flag `[allowFiltering]="true"`, `[filterSettings]`, `[allowPaging]`, `[allowGrouping]`, `[allowReordering]`, and `coesia-accordion` wrappers added without an explicit spec requirement — they are opt-in. Free-text search must be an external `<input>` + `signal` + `computed()` filtered dataSource; flag `[toolbar]="['Search']"` / `'Search'` entries in grid toolbars.
- Angular colors: flag any raw hex string literal (`'#xxxxxx'`, `'#xxx'`) in `.ts`, `.html`, or `.scss` outside the narrow exceptions documented in `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Colors — source of truth & TS usage". Templates and `.scss` must use theme classes (`text-gray-900`, `bg-primary`, `text-grasgreen-500`, etc.); `.ts` files (chart configs, SVG/canvas, dynamic style bindings) must import the `Colors` enum from `@coesia/common` and reference enum members (`Colors.DarkGrey700`, `Colors.GrasGreen500`, …). The coesia-theme SCSS files (`libs/frontend/themes/coesia-theme/styles/colors/**`) are the single source of truth — if the needed value is missing from `Colors`, request adding/aligning the enum entry rather than accepting an inline hex. Allowed exceptions: the `Colors` enum file itself, theme SCSS, and `*.mocks.ts` seed payloads (still hoisted to a named `const`).
- Angular borders: when Figma shows a panel/card/KPI tile/chart container/grid wrapper/badge with an outline, the implementation must be `border border-2 rounded-2` (or `border border-2 rounded-pill` for pill badges). Flag `border` without `border-2` on card-style surfaces (1px is invisible on white in this theme — looks borderless), and flag one-off `border-grey-300` / `border-grey-200` / `border-grey-400` / inline `border-color` on neutral borders (drop the color class, rely on Bootstrap's default `--bs-border-color`). See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Borders".
- Angular legend pills: flag any chip-style legend pill (`border border-2 rounded-pill`) whose inner label `<span>` is missing `fw-bold`, including the section label preceding the pills (e.g. `Righe:` / `Celle:` / `Status:`). Bold is mandatory even when Figma extracts `font-medium` / `Roboto:Medium`. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Legend pills / chip badges".
- Angular `::ng-deep`: flag any occurrence of `:host ::ng-deep` / `::ng-deep` in component SCSS. `::ng-deep` is deprecated and forbidden in new code. Every legitimate case has a class-based replacement (Syncfusion column `[customAttributes]="{ class: '…' }"`, `classList.add('<bg-utility>')` in `rowDataBound`, theme classes on projected content). See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Never use `:host ::ng-deep`".
- Angular dynamic styling: flag any pattern where a TS callback (Syncfusion `rowDataBound`, `queryCellInfo`, similar) adds a component-local class (`xmate-row-added`, `xmate-row-modified`, …) that is then matched in scoped SCSS with `background-color: $… !important` / `color: $… !important`. The Bootstrap utility class (`bg-grasgreen-100`, `bg-yellow-100`, `bg-grey-100`, `text-*`, `fw-*`) must be applied directly via `classList.add(<utility>)` so no scoped SCSS rule with `!important` is needed. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Dynamic styling — push utility classes, do not write scoped SCSS".
- Angular third-party overrides: flag any `(queryCellInfo)` / `(headerCellInfo)` / `(dataBound)` handler, custom `[customAttributes]` class, SCSS rule for an `.e-*` selector, or `cssClass` binding whose sole effect is to push a property the third-party widget already provides by default (Syncfusion `.e-rowcell` already ships `position: relative`; `<ejs-grid>` headers already use flex; Bootstrap form controls already carry their spacing). Require the PR to prove the override is visibly necessary by removing it and reloading — if the visual is unchanged, the handler/class is dead code and must be dropped. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Verify third-party defaults before adding overrides".
- Angular generated theme utilities: flag any fixed-px `width` / `height` / `min-*` / `max-*` / `font-size` / `transform: rotate(...)` / `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, …)` rule in component `.scss` whose value is already emitted by `libs/frontend/themes/coesia-theme/styles/mixins.scss` (`h-Npx`, `w-Npx`, `wh-Npx`, `min-h-Npx`, `max-w-Npx`, `fs-N`, `rotate-N`, `auto-fit-grid-Npx`, …). The class must be applied on the template element instead. Common example: `.my-dot { width: 14px; height: 14px; }` → `wh-14px`. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Generated theme utilities + component classes (do NOT re-implement)".
- Angular composite theme classes: flag template markup that hand-rolls a recurring primitive (legend dot, status indicator, etc.) with a utility stack when a domain composite class already encodes the same intent. Example: `<span class="wh-14px rounded-circle flex-shrink-0 bg-grasgreen-500"></span>` → `<span class="dot dot-xl bg-grasgreen-500"></span>` (see `libs/frontend/themes/coesia-theme/styles/indicators.scss` for `.dot` + size modifiers `dot-sm`/`dot-md`/`dot-lg`/`dot-xl`). Re-scan that directory each review — the composite family grows over time. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Generated theme utilities + component classes (do NOT re-implement)".
- Angular mock data placement: flag any mock literal (`*Mock*` arrays, Figma demo payloads, hardcoded `readonly`/`const` seed data) declared inside a component, directive, service, or facade. Mocks must live in a sibling `mocks/` folder (or `<domain>/models/mocks/` if none exists), one `.mocks.ts` file per consumer, exported as `*_MOCK` constants or `build*Mock(...)` factories. See `.github/instructions/angular.instructions.md` → "Mock data placement".
- TypeScript `readonly` fields: flag any class field whose reference is never reassigned after initialization but is declared without `readonly`. Hard-required on every `inject(...)` result, signal factory (`signal`, `computed`, `linkedSignal`, `model`, `model.required`), input/output factory (`input`, `input.required`, `output`), query factory (`viewChild*`, `contentChild*`), resource handle (`resource`, `httpResource`, `rxResource`, `rxGuardedResource`), and any constant assigned in the field initializer or constructor and never written elsewhere. `readonly` locks the reference, not the value — writable signals stay writable. See `.github/instructions/typescript.instructions.md` → "Readonly fields".
- .NET solution governance: enforce the rules defined in `.github/copilot-instructions.md` (`Solutions/Monorepo.sln` synchronized first, `.slnf` files synchronized with it, and one `.csproj` per folder).

## Feedback style

- Prefer actionable comments with rationale and a suggested fix.
- Avoid requesting large refactors unless necessary for correctness, security, or maintainability.

## General coding practices

- Prefer small, focused functions/components; call out SRP/SOLID violations when they cause real complexity.
- DRY: avoid code duplication when a shared helper is simple and improves clarity.
- Naming: verify class/method/variable names are descriptive and still accurate after refactors. Names should reflect the value's actual role, not a broader concept it merely helps construct.
- Avoid magic numbers/strings; use constants or enums where it improves readability.
- Fail fast: validate external inputs at boundaries; no silent failures or ignored exceptions.
- Error handling: require meaningful error messages; avoid swallowing errors.
- Comments: outdated comments are worse than no comments—ensure they remain accurate; prefer documenting “why”, not “what”. Flag empty comments and generic TODO/question comments that do not explain the remaining work or point to a tracking item.
- Comments vs code drift: when a code change and its nearby comment disagree, do not recommend reverting the code solely to make it match the comment. First judge whether the code change itself appears correct; if it does, recommend updating or removing the stale comment. Only suggest reverting code when there is separate evidence that the new behavior is wrong or that the comment captures a required contract, invariant, or externally observable behavior.
- Performance: flag obvious big-O issues, accidental N+1 patterns, unnecessary repeated work, or heavy allocations in hot paths.
- Follow existing patterns in the codebase; avoid drive-by rewrites.
- Don’t ask for fixing unrelated warnings unless directly caused by the change.
